summary_one_line: Attribute-type token must equal exactly 'ID' (2 bytes) while not matching the longer CDATA/IDREFS/IDREF/ENTITY string literals.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Inside a DTD ATTLIST attribute-type position, the input must present the two-character token 'I''D' (RAW=='I' AND NXT(1)=='D') after the earlier CMP5/CMP6 literal tests for 'CDATA', 'IDREFS', 'IDREF', 'ENTITY' have all failed — i.e. the token is 'ID' and not a longer ID-prefixed keyword.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 10/10 trials and actually hits the true side (avg hitcount=6); its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose attribute-type bytes get Hamming-closer to the matched literals, letting it converge a token to exactly the 2-byte 'ID' that satisfies (RAW=='I')&&(NXT(1)=='D') at line 6026.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (same I2S, no value_profile) blocks in 9/10 trials with avg hitcount=0 and never takes the true side (d=1 L6026 shows L T=0); lacking the partial-match CMP_MAP gradient it cannot incrementally steer the attribute-type token to land on the short 'ID' literal between the longer keyword alternatives.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between value_profile_cmplog and cmplog is value_profile, so the divergence is attributable to the CMP_MAP gradient: value_profile's Hamming/prefix-distance feedback gives intermediate reward as a candidate attribute-type token approaches 'ID', whereas cmplog's edge-only feedback sees the comparison as all-or-nothing and gets no gradient toward the 2-byte literal.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the ID attribute-type test requiring RAW=='I' and NXT(1)=='D', reached only after the preceding string-literal alternatives fail.
The branch sits after CDATA / IDREFS / IDREF literal comparisons in xmlParseAttributeType, so the token must be exactly 'ID' and not a longer ID-prefixed keyword.
The winner value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in all trials and actually exercises the true side.
The winner hits the branch (nonzero hitcount) while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=6  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line the winner takes the true side and the loser shows zero true hits.
The loser is cmplog, the winner value_profile_cmplog, so the differentiating technique is value_profile.
value_profile_cmplog feedback adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's signal.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient, not I2SRandReplace, so the dash-row lineage check is not the applicable signal here: both fuzzers in this pair (cmplog and value_profile_cmplog) carry I2SRandReplace, so I2S cannot explain the divergence; the only technique delta is value_profile, evidenced by the [delta: value_profile] tag in DECISIVE PAIRS and the CMP_MAP-bucket feedback line in MECHANISM CONTEXT. Lineage was therefore not run as it would not isolate the value_profile contribution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic ATTLIST harness gating on the 2-byte 'ID' attribute-type literal where value_profile_cmplog and cmplog resolve the branch at statistically indistinguishable rates (no value_profile advantage).

weakest_evidence_point: Whether value_profile's gradient operates on this short 2-byte 'ID' comparison specifically, versus simply on the surrounding longer CMP5/CMP6 literal tests that all share the prefix, is inferred from source structure rather than a per-comparison CMP_MAP trace.
